description_long: This is a set of three identical, rectangular apartment buildings with a minimalist, utilitarian design. The buildings are constructed from gray concrete, giving them a stark, industrial appearance. Each building features a flat roof and a symmetrical facade with two rows of square windows, each row containing two windows, for a total of four windows per building. The ground floor of each building has a single, small entrance door and a larger, garage-like door, both metallic and weathered. The sides and rear of the buildings are plain, lacking windows or decorative elements, emphasizing their functional nature. The overall look is reminiscent of Brutalist architecture, with a focus on raw materials and geometric forms.
description: Three identical, gray concrete apartment buildings with flat roofs, symmetrical window arrangements, and utilitarian entrances. The design is stark and industrial, with minimal decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing three buildings with symmetrical windows and metallic doors on the ground floor.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain, windowless sides of the buildings, emphasizing their rectangular shape.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the identical window and door arrangement as the front, with visible weathering on the doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the plain, unadorned sides of the buildings.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a symmetrical grid pattern, with two rows of two square windows each, evenly spaced on the facade of each building.
